edges.description: Collagen gene defects reduce, mislocalize, or dysfunctionally assemble collagen VI/XII matrix.
edges.source: Collagen VI and XII gene defects

edges.description: Matrix deficiency produces abnormal muscle connective tissue and muscle-fiber pathology.
Collagen matrix deficiency promotes downstream mitochondrial permeability transition, myofiber apoptosis, and impaired autophagy.
edges.target: Mitochondrion-mediated myofiber apoptosis and impaired autophagy

edges.description: Matrix defects in muscle and connective tissue produce the characteristic mixed contracture/hyperlaxity pattern.
Progressive skeletal-muscle involvement affects respiratory muscles and respiratory function.
Mitochondrial dysfunction, apoptosis, and impaired autophagy contribute to downstream muscle-fiber pathology.
edges.source: Mitochondrion-mediated myofiber apoptosis and impaired autophagy

edges.description: Myofiber apoptosis and impaired cellular quality control contribute to skeletal muscle weakness.
edges.source: Mitochondrion-mediated myofiber apoptosis and impaired autophagy

edges.description: Myofiber and muscle-connective tissue pathology contributes to generalized weakness.
Muscle involvement produces a dystrophic biochemical injury marker.
Proximal contractures manifest as flexion contractures.
Distal hyperlaxity manifests as wrist hypermobility.
Distal hyperlaxity manifests as finger joint laxity.
Respiratory muscle involvement causes respiratory insufficiency or failure.

edges.source: Cyclosporin A mitochondrial permeability transition modulation
edges.target: Mitochondrion-mediated myofiber apoptosis and impaired autophagy

nodes.description: Cyclosporin A has been evaluated in an open pilot trial for collagen VI myopathies as a downstream disease-modifying therapy that desensitizes the mitochondrial permeability transition pore, with short-term normalization of muscle-biopsy mitochondrial dysfunction and apoptosis markers. This remains preliminary pharmacotherapy rather than established standard care.
nodes.id: Cyclosporin A mitochondrial permeability transition modulation

evidence.reference_title: Cyclosporin A corrects mitochondrial dysfunction and muscle apoptosis in patients with collagen VI myopathies.
